data.title: Luxury's fourth quarter false dawn
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 up 0.5%
  • A&D stocks lead gainers
  • Gold hits $3,000/oz
  • Wall St futures higher

LUXURY'S FOURTH QUARTER FALSE DAWN

One of the key themes from Europe's fourth quarter earnings season was signs that the bloc's luxury sector might be coming back to life after a disappointing year, but the recovery might have stalled in Q1, according to JPMorgan.

"Our analysis ... suggests more muted trends for the sector since the beginning of the year, with notably a largely broad-based step down in consumer engagement in February," JPM strategists write.

"This is also consistent with our view ... that Q1 25 will show no further improvement vs Q4, but rather in some cases some sequential growth moderation."

Q4 results from across the sector were mostly positive, from high-end Hermes , to turnaround play Burberry .

But JPM's analysis highlights Burberry, along with Kering's Gucci, as having had "particularly soft performances" this quarter. They have placed both companies on "negative catalyst watch".

(Samuel Indyk)

*****

FRIDAY'S OTHER LIVE MARKETS POSTS:

STOXX UP BUT HEADING FOR BIGGEST WEEKLY DROP IN 3 MONTHS CLICK HERE

EUROPE BEFORE THE BELL: FUTURES HIGHER, STOCKS STILL SET FOR WEEKLY LOSS CLICK HERE

MARKETS COME UP FOR AIR BUT TARIFF THREATS LURK CLICK HERE

data.title: Emerald Holding Sees FY25 Rev $450M-$460M >EEX
Casablanca Group Expects Loss Of HK$9.0 Mln To HK$10.0 Mln For 2024
data.description:
Casablanca Group Ltd :

  • EXPECTS LOSS OF HK$9.0 MILLION TO HK$10.0 MILLION FOR 2024

  • INCREASE IN LOSS DUE TO DECLINE IN OVERALL SALES IN FY
